div.head: 概覽
div.p: 29號窟僅刊刻了一部佛經的四卷，33號窟則將六種不同佛經或其片段合在一起。 刻工先從d壁開始刊刻《灌頂經》最後一卷，即卷十二《佛說灌頂拔除過罪生死得度經》。完成之後， 接著從第四十八行起刻該經卷十一《佛說灌頂隨願往生十方淨土經》。每卷五千餘字，是此窟最長的三部佛經之二。開始刊刻卷十一時，每一行開頭都增加一個字， 並且縮短行距。儘管如此，仍未能在狹小的壁面上刻完卷十一，不得不在距後牆e壁11釐米處停止， 然後到e壁繼續刻了六行。在最後一行，即第六行處，緊接著刻了篇幅短小的《佛說六門陀羅尼經》， 該經到第九行處結束。第十至三十二行接續的是完整而相對較短的《佛臨般涅槃略說遺教經》。此壁刻經所佔寬度僅70釐米，其後再無經文。 e壁剩餘的約170釐米寬的壁面可能並不適於刊刻。今天這部分壁面毀損嚴重。
f壁預備刻字的打磨區域照例 始於距e壁交界線4至6釐米處，但刻經面在36至48釐米之後才開始， 寬僅137釐米。前六行最長，內容為《佛說出家功德經》。最後，布局變得更加穩定。 後續的第七至六十五行是《金剛般若波羅蜜經》全文，又是另一篇5076字的長文。刻經面結束於距入口31至33釐米處，間距合理。

div.head: 位置

list.item: 該窟位於南崖A區，高於現今路面約51釐米。
f壁方向：326°。

div.p: 一段寬17釐米的立面將其與西側的32號龕隔開，龕中有一鋪倚坐佛像；東側是34號龕， 33號窟和34號龕之間是一段立面，即33–1號龕， 龕中刻有四排千佛雕像，寬度為103釐米（）。

figure.head: 33號窟平面圖；雷魯卡繪製
Ground plan of cave 33; drawing Lukas Ledderose

div.head: 形制
div.p: 方形窟，雙層窟口；門檻下沉；高約5釐米，寬約40釐米。平面呈長方形，寬度大於進深，向內漸寬。窟內地面前部較平，後部較前部低約6釐米。窟頂平坦，由窟口向內略微擡升。

list.item: d壁：前部高221釐米，後部高233釐米，寬190釐米。
e壁：西端高233釐米，中央高245釐米，東端高235釐米，寬252釐米。
f壁：前部高222釐米，後部高235釐米，寬205釐米。
窟口寬：209釐米。
f壁入口一側及崖壁被鑿掉一部分，以便為鑿刻相鄰的33–1號千佛龕。入口安裝了鐵柵門。2011年修建的長屋簷也覆蓋了該窟窟口。

figure.head: 33號窟刻經區概覽，自右往左依次為d壁、e壁和f壁
Overview of the fields of script in cave 33, on walls d, e, and f, from right to left

list.item: 打磨面前距入口約6釐米，上緣距窟頂約3釐米，下緣始於距地面約20釐米處，後距e壁約4釐米。
刻經面原為高185釐米、寬162釐米的縱長方形。前距入口約12釐米，上緣距窟頂後部約12至20釐米不等，下緣距窟地面25釐米，後距後牆約11釐米。從第四十八行開始，每行行首加刻一字。

row.cell: 約0.6至1.0釐米；第48行以後變小，約0.6釐米
約0.5釐米；第48行以後變小，約0.2釐米

div.head: 保存狀況
div.p: 不良。門檻上方約139釐米處，有一個呈三角形的凹陷區域，切入刻經面約6釐米，直到刻經第三行的位置。 距地面約118釐米處，數道平行的水平裂縫橫穿整個壁面。下方壁面有幾處大塊區域內的文字已無法辨識。 地面上方約155釐米處，有一塊較大的水平區域壁面剝落，高13至23釐米，從入口向內延伸約92釐米。

row.cell: ［佛說灌頂拔除過罪生］死得［度］經；標識《佛說灌頂拔除過罪生死得度經》的開端，該經為《灌頂經》第十二卷；根據第48行推斷，未刻卷次。經名僅“死”、“得”和“經”三字尚可辨識。
［佛說灌頂拔除過罪生死得度經］；不可見，根據臥佛院所存刻經結尾復原而成；標識《佛說灌頂拔除過罪生死得度經》的結束。
佛說灌頂隨願往生十方淨土經；標識《佛說灌頂隨願往生十方淨土經》的開端，該經為《灌頂經》第十一卷。未刻卷次。

figure.head: A 區33號窟；2009年拍攝
Cave 33 in section A; photograph taken in 2009

list.item: 打磨面距離d壁約5釐米，上緣距窟頂約12至13釐米，下緣距地面約29釐米，後部距離f壁約87釐米。
刻經面為高200釐米、寬70釐米的縱長方形。距離d壁約11釐米，距f壁170釐米，上緣距窟頂約14至16釐米，下緣距窟地面約30釐米。壁面的東部未打磨，如今毀損嚴重。

row.cell: 約0.5釐米；第10行以後0.5至0.7釐米
約0.1釐米；第10行以後0.2至0.4釐米

div.head: 保存狀況
div.p: 不良。地面至上方124釐米處，字跡難以辨識。地面上方約151釐米處，一塊高約14至28釐米的水平區域橫貫刻經面，這一區域很潮濕。該窟開鑿者可能在開窟過程中遇到滲水區後，決定不再往崖壁更深處開鑿。

row.cell: 波利［富樓］那 遮利三曼阤……；接d壁第79行的經文。
佛說灌頂［隨願］往［生十方］淨［土］經，七字可辨識；標識《佛說灌頂隨願往生十方淨土經》的結束。
六門阤［羅尼經］；三字可見，無“佛說”二字；標識《佛說六門陀羅尼經》的開端。
佛說六門阤羅尼經；有“佛說”二字；標識《佛說六門陀羅尼經》的結束。
佛臨般涅槃略說遺教經；標識《佛臨般涅槃略說遺教經》的開端。
［佛臨般涅槃略說遺教經］；不可見，根據臥佛院所存刻經結尾復原而成；標識《佛臨般涅槃略說遺教經》的結束。

list.item: 打磨面前部距e壁約4至6釐米，上緣距窟頂約4至11釐米，下緣距地面約11至17釐米，距入口約20釐米。
刻經面始於距e壁僅36至48釐米處，寬度為137釐米。上緣距窟頂約11至15釐米，與入口相距31至33釐米。前六行高187釐米，第七至六十五行行首較前低三個字，高181釐米。距地面15至23釐米。

row.cell: 18 釐米（第1至6行）；20 釐米（第7至65行）

div.head: 保存狀況
div.p: 狀況不一。石窟後半部分：窟頂下方的區域保存狀況最佳，中部毀損，其下保存狀況尚可，再往下的部分文字已不可見。石窟前半部分：窟頂以下至地面上方約140釐米的區域保存狀況最佳，下方四分之一的壁面已完全漫漶。

row.cell: 佛說［出家］功德經；標識《佛說出家功德經》的開端。
視世善惡恣；此部經最後幾個可辨識的字。該行接下去應刻至“歡喜奉行，作礼而去”（已磨滅）。依照臥佛院其他刻經的形式慣例，《佛說出家功德經》經名可能在結尾處又重複刊刻一次。
金剛般［若波羅蜜經］；標識《金剛般若波羅蜜經》的開端。
如夢、幻、泡、影，［如］露；此部經最後幾個可辨識的字。
［金剛般若波羅蜜經］；不可見，根據臥佛院所存刻經結尾復原而成；標識《金剛般若波羅蜜經》的結束。

ab.abbr: 雷德侯 任婧 蔡穗玲

div.head: 討論
div.bibl:
胡文和、李官智第20頁
胡文和和李官智僅列出該窟《灌頂經》卷十一和卷十二，《般若波羅蜜多心經》的刊刻位置描述有誤。

彭家勝第6頁
彭家勝列出該窟的高、寬、深三維數據，進而計算出石窟體積為8.987立方米。除《灌頂經》卷十一外，他未能正確辨識該窟其他刻經。他指出d壁的保存狀況“較好”，而e壁和f壁“風化嚴重”。

胡文和第8頁
胡文和重複了他與李官智1986年論文中的信息（第20頁）。他列出該窟的三維數據（同彭家勝第6頁），但並未計算其體積。

李良、鄧之金 第40頁
李良和鄧之金認為，該窟開鑿於唐代。他們描述了該窟形制，並測量了尺寸，還測量了刻經區的尺寸並對所刻經文做了如下判斷：
d壁：高180釐米、寬166釐米，刻有《灌頂經》卷十一“隨願［往生］十方淨土”，計79行，每行115字，共9085字，部分風化。
e壁：高200釐米、寬67釐米，所刻經文計35行，每行115字，共4025字。因風化嚴重，難以確定經題。
f壁：高200釐米、寬140釐米，刻有一部經，計65行，每行80字，共5200字。該經誤判作《般若波羅蜜多心經》，大部分風化。
他們將該窟的整體保存狀況描述為“風化嚴重”。

成都考古發現第364－365、405頁
報告說明了洞窟的位置、形制和尺寸。其中提到了一些自然裂縫和後來出現在正立面的人為添加物。
該報告確定了各壁刻經的經題和版本。關於保存狀況，報告指出d壁的上半部分靠近入口處風蝕嚴重， e壁僅右上角一處方形區域內的刻字尚且清晰。f壁下半部分和與e壁相鄰的區域風化嚴重。

曹丹、于春 11
曹丹和于春提供石壁的尺寸和表面積，並根據拓片數出所存刻經的字數：d壁2596字，e壁475字，f壁682字。兩位學者辨識出《灌頂經》卷十一與卷十二以及《金剛經》。

div.head: Overview
div.p:
Whereas four scrolls of only one sutra fill cave 29, six different texts, or parts thereof, have been strung together in cave 33. The engravers started in the first column of wall d with the last scroll of the Consecration Sutra , scroll 12, the Consecration Sutra Spoken by the Buddha that Rescues from Sin and Enables Salvation from Birth and Death (Fo shuo guanding bachu guozui shengsi dedu jing 佛說灌頂拔除過罪生死得度經 ). Having completed this scroll, the engravers continued in column 48 with scroll 11, the Consecration Sutra Spoken by the Buddha Concerning Rebirth in the Pure Lands of the Ten Directions According to One’s Wish (Fo shuo guanding suiyuan wangsheng shifang jingtu jing 佛說灌頂隨願往生十方淨土經 ). With more than five thousand characters each, these scrolls are two of the three longest texts in the cave. When the carvers began with scroll 11, they increased the number of characters in all columns by one additional character at the top, and they also shrunk the distance between the vertical columns. Despite these efforts, they could not fit the entire scroll 11 on the short wall, but had to stop 11 cm before the corner with the rear wall e, and then they had to continue around the corner for another six columns. In the last of these, column 6, they then immediately began with the short
Dhāraṇī Sutra of the Six Gates Spoken by the Buddha , which comes to an quick end in column 9. In columns 10 to 32 follows the entire, relatively short, Deathbed Injunction Sutra . On wall e, this sutra completes the field of script, which is only 70 cm wide. The ca. 170 cm remaining on the wall may not have been suitable for further carving. Today this part is heavily damaged.

figure.head: 33號窟平面圖；雷魯卡繪製
Ground plan of cave 33; drawing Lukas Ledderose

div.p:
On wall f, the prepared polished area begins as usual ca. 4 to 6 cm from the corner with wall e, but the field of script starts only after 36 to 48 cm, and it is only 137 cm wide. The first six columns, which are longer than the rest, contain the Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on the Merit and Virtue in Renouncing the Householder’s Life . Finally, the layout becomes more stable. In columns 7 to 65 follows the complete text of the
Diamond Perfection of Insight Sutra , with 5,076 characters, another long text. The field of script ends at a reasonable distance of 31 to 33 cm from the entrance.

div.head: Location

list.item: Southern slope, area A, ca. 51 cm above present walkway.
Direction: wall f 326˚.

div.ab: Separated to the west from shrine 32, which has a seated Buddha with pendant legs, by a section of the facade,17 cm in width; to the east follows shrine 34; between cave 33 and shrine 34 is a section of the facade, shrine 33-1, with four rows of Thousand Buddhas, at a width of 103 cm (fig. ).

div.head: Shape
div.p: Box shape, a reveal on three sides of the opening; sunken threshold; height ca. 5 cm, width ca. 40 cm. The ground plan is a rectangle, broader than deep, widening toward the rear; the front part of the floor is flat; the rear part is ca. 6 cm lower. The flat ceiling rises slightly towards the rear.

figure.head: A 區33號窟；2007年拍攝
Cave 33 in section A; photograph taken in 2007

list.item: Wall d: height at the front 221 cm, at the rear 233 cm, width 190 cm.
Wall e: height at western corner 233 cm, at midpoint 245 cm, at eastern corner 235 cm, width 252 cm.
Wall f: height at the front 222 cm, at the rear 235 cm, width 205 cm.
width of opening: 209 cm.

Part of the cliff face and of wall f at the entrance were cut back to make room for the adjacent Thousand Buddhas shrine 33-1. A steel grill is fixed in the western half of the entrance opening. The long roof built in 2011 extends also above this cave.
The polished area begins ca. 6 cm from the entrance, and ca. 3 cm below the ceiling; it ends ca. 20 cm above the floor and ca. 4 cm from the rear wall e.

The original field of script was a vertical rectangle, 185 x 162 cm. The distance from the entrance is ca. 12 cm, the distance from the ceiling varies from 12 cm to 20 cm at the rear; the distance of the lowest row from the floor is 25 cm; the distance to the rear wall is ca. 11 cm. After column 47, the columns begin at the top with one extra character each.

row.cell: ca. 0.6–1.0 cm; tighter after column 48, ca. 0.6 cm
ca. 0.5 cm; tighter after column 48, ca. 0.2 cm

div.head: State of preservation
div.p: Poor. At a height of ca. 139 cm above the threshold, a triangular indentation of the wall cuts ca. 6 cm into the field of script as far as the third column. Several parallel horizontal cracks run through the entire wall at a height of ca. 118 cm above the floor. In the wall portion below, large areas are illegible. Ca. 155 cm above the floor, a larger horizontal flaked zone, 13–23 cm in height, extends ca. 92 cm from the entrance.

row.cell:
［佛說灌頂拔除過罪生］死得［度］經; refers to the beginning of the Consecration Sutra Spoken by the Buddha that rescues from Sin and Enables Salvation from Birth and Death (Fo shuo guanding bachu guozui shengsi de du
佛說灌頂拔除過罪生死得度), which is scroll 12 of the Consecration Sutra (Guanding jing 灌頂經); by analogy with column 48, it is assumed that no scroll number was indicated. Only the three characters si 死, de 得 and jing 經 are recognizable.

［佛說灌頂拔除過罪生死得度經］; not visible, reconstruction by analogy with the extant endings of other carved sutras; refers to the end of the Consecration Sutra Spoken by the Buddha that rescues from Sin and Enables Salvation from Birth and Death .

佛說灌頂隨願往生十方淨土經; refers to the beginning of the Consecration Sutra Spoken by the Buddha Concerning Rebirth in the Pure Land of the Ten Directions According to One’s Wish (Fo shuo guanding suiyuan wangsheng shifang jingtu jing 佛說灌頂隨願往生十方淨土經), which is scroll 11 of the Consecration Sutra (Guanding jing 灌頂經); no scroll number is indicated.

list.item: The polished area begins ca. 5 cm from wall d and ca. 12 to 13 cm below the ceiling; it ends ca. 29 cm above the floor and ca. 87 cm from wall f.

The field of script is a vertical rectangle, 200 x 70 cm. The distance from wall d is ca. 11 cm and from wall f ca. 170 cm; the distance from the ceiling measures 14–16 cm and the distance of the lowest row from the floor ca. 30 cm. The eastern part of the wall is not polished. Today it is heavily damaged.

div.head: State of preservation
div.p: Poor. Up to a height of 124 cm above the floor, the characters are hardly readable. At a height of ca. 151 cm above the floor, a horizontal zone, 14–28 cm high, which is now moist, runs through the field of script. The masons may have encountered the damp area and may then have decided not to cut any deeper into the cliff.

row.cell: 波利［富樓］那 遮利三曼阤……; continuation from wall d, column 79.
佛說灌頂［隨願］往［生十方］淨［土］經, seven characters visible; refers to the end of the Consecration Sutra Spoken by the Buddha Concerning Rebirth in the Pure Land of the Ten Directions According to One’s Wish .

六門阤［羅尼經］; three characters visible, without the two characters “佛說”; refers to the beginning of the
Dhāraṇī Sutra of the Six Gates Spoken by the Buddha (Fo shuo liumen tuoluoni jing 佛說六門陀羅尼經)

佛說六門阤羅尼經; with the two characters “佛說”; refers to the end of the
Dhāraṇī Sutra of the Six Gates Spoken by the Buddha .

佛臨般涅槃略說遺教經; refers to the beginning of the Sutra of the Deathbed Injunction Briefly Spoken by the Buddha on the Point of Parinirvāṇa
(Fo lin boniepan lueshuo yijiao jing 佛臨般涅槃略說遺教經).

［佛臨般涅槃略說遺教經］; not visible; reconstruction by analogy with the extant endings of other carved sutras; refers to the end of the Sutra of Deathbed Injunction Briefly Spoken by the Buddha on the Point of Parinirvāṇa
(Fo lin boniepan lueshuo yijiao jing
佛臨般涅槃略說遺教經).

list.item: The polished area begins ca. 4–6 cm from wall e and ca. 4–11 cm below the ceiling. It ends ca. 11–17 cm above the floor and ca. 20 cm from the entrance.

The field of script begins only 36–48 cm from wall e; its width is 137 cm. The distance from the ceiling is 11–15 cm and the distance above the floor is 15–23 cm. The distance to the entrance is 31–33 cm. The first six columns have a height of 187 cm. Columns 7 to 65 begin three rows lower; their height is 181 cm.

row.cell: beginning from column 7, none in columns 1–6

div.head: State of preservation
div.p: Uneven. Rear half: best in the area under the ceiling, worn zone in the center, fair zone below; lower area not visible; front half: best in the area from the ceiling down to a height of ca. 140 cm above the floor; surface completely worn off in lower front quadrant.

row.cell: 佛說［出家］功德經; refers to the beginning of the Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on the Merit and Virtue in Renouncing the Householder’s Life (Fo shuo chujia gongde jing 佛說出家功德經).

視世善惡恣 are the last visible characters. The text in that column may continue to the last sentence of the sutra: 歡喜奉行，作礼而去 (not visible). By analogy with other sutra carvings, the title 佛說出家功德經, Sutra on the Merit and Virtue in Renouncing the House­holder’s Life Spoken by the Buddha , may have been repeated at the end of the engraving.
金剛般［若波羅蜜經］; refers to the beginning of the Diamond Perfection of Insight Sutra (Jin’gang bore boluomi jing 金剛般若波羅蜜經).
如夢、幻、泡、影，［如］露 are the last visible characters.

note.p:
For this cave, Hu and Li list only scrolls 11 and 12 of the
Consecration Sutra
灌頂經 and mention incorrectly a second carving of the Heart Sutra (Bore boluomiduo xin jing
般若波羅蜜多心經) in this cave.

Peng gives measurements of the three dimensions of the cave and computes its volume as 8,987 cubic meters. He misidentifies the sutra texts, with the exception of scroll 11 of the Consecration Sutra
灌頂經. He notes that the state of preservation of wall d is “rather good, 較好,” and says that walls e and f are “severely weathered, 風化嚴重. ”
Hu repeats the information from 20. He gives measurements of the three dimensions of the cave (the same as those in 6), but he does not compute its volume.

Li and Deng believe that this cave was carved in the Tang dynasty. They describe the shape of the cave and give its measurements. They also give measurements of the fields of script and identify some sutras as follows:

list.item:
wall d: 180 x 166 cm, carved with the Consecration Sutra
灌頂經, scroll 11, 隨願［往生］十方淨土, in 79 columns, with 115 characters in each column, 9,085 in total; partly weathered.
wall e: 200 x 67 cm; sutras carved in 35 columns, with 115 characters in each column, 4,025 in total. The title of sutras cannot be identified, because they are severely weathered.

wall f: 200 x 140 cm; a sutra in 65 columns, with 80 characters in each column, 5,200 in total; incorrectly identified as the Heart Sutra
般若波羅蜜多心經; largely weathered.

note.p: They describe the overall state of preservation of this cave as “severely weathered, 風化嚴重.”
The authors present the location, shape and measurements of the cave and mention some natural cracks and later changes to the facade.

The titles and versions of sutras on each wall are identified. On the state of preservation, they note that the upper part of wall d, near the entrance, is severely eroded; on wall e only the characters in a square area in the upper right corner are still clear; on wall f, the lower part and the area adjacent with wall e are severely weathered.

Cao and Yu give measurements of the walls, their surface areas, and based on rubbings the number of extant characters, wall d: 2,596; wall e: 475; wall f: 682. They identify scrolls 11 and 12 of the
Consecration Sutra and the Diamond Sutra .
